GAO Report, , , directed the Comptroller General, within 3 years after , to transmit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report regarding the impact of regulations on the competitiveness of pipelines and to recommend whether to continue, revise, or sunset such regulations.Pub. children.text: to recognize and preserve the inherent advantage of each mode of transportation;
to promote safe, adequate, economical, and efficient transportation;
to encourage sound economic conditions in transportation, including sound economic conditions among carriers;
to encourage the establishment and maintenance of reasonable rates for transportation without unreasonable discrimination or unfair or destructive competitive practices;
to cooperate with each State and the officials of each State on transportation matters; and
to encourage fair wages and working conditions in the transportation industry.

children.heading: .—Administration To Carry Out Policy
children.label: b .—Administration To Carry Out Policy
children.text: This part shall be administered and enforced to carry out the policy of this section.
